summary.classification_reason: The work changes first-contact startup guidance, managed payloads, and tests, but can be completed as one bounded package lane without creating a separate epic hierarchy.
summary.not_direct_reason: It requires package workflow ingestion, managed payload alignment, tests, validation, and closeout dogfooding.
summary.not_epic_reason: The accepted intent is a focused workflow-bootstrap improvement, not a multi-release product program.
summary.adaptive_assurance: high because startup adapters and workflow bootstrap text shape future agent behavior and can cause broad misuse if vague or misleading.

references.label: Make managed workflow bootstrap CLI-first and classification-driven

lane_impacts.summary: Ingest #647 into checked-in planning, classify the work, and record bounded decomposition before implementation.
Make the managed AGENTS.md workflow fence visibly route agents to the CLI-first bootstrap while staying machine-detectable and minimal.
Rewrite installed and packaged .agentic-workspace/WORKFLOW.md as a CLI-first bootstrap router with Markdown fallback for weak agents.
Align startup/config/defaults/report guidance and tests where needed so structured surfaces point agents through classification before broad work.
Run focused tests, maintainer surface refresh, planning checks, dogfooding closeout, and archive the plan if complete.

findings.title: Stale pointer recognition fixed during dogfooding
findings.summary: The memory package still recognized only the old workspace pointer block and reported a fresh init as stale.
findings.evidence: Focused workspace report tests failed until packages/memory/src/repo_memory_bootstrap/_installer_shared.py used the same visible workspace pointer block as the root package.
findings.risk if unchanged: Fresh installs with the new thin visible fence would produce false maintenance pressure.
findings.suggested action: Fixed in this lane and covered by package memory installer tests.

findings.title: Stale AGENTS prose checker fixed during dogfooding
findings.summary: The planning surface checker still required old startup-order prose in AGENTS.md.
findings.evidence: check_planning_surfaces.py warned after AGENTS.md was intentionally reduced to the managed fence.
findings.risk if unchanged: The package would tell agents to keep duplicating startup prose in host repo instructions.
findings.suggested action: Fixed in this lane and covered by maintainer-surface and planning checker tests.
